components.role: Schmitt trigger comparator — generates square wave by switching between high/low output at threshold voltages
Integrator — converts square wave to triangle wave by integrating the constant-current charge/discharge
Rate control pot — sets LFO frequency by controlling integrator charge rate
Integrator capacitor — core timing element, sets frequency range with Rrate
Schmitt trigger hysteresis resistor — positive feedback, sets switching thresholds
Schmitt trigger hysteresis resistor — sets amplitude of triangle output
Integrator input resistor — works with Rrate to set charge current
Depth control — attenuates LFO output amplitude for modulation depth
Bias divider upper — Vcc/2 reference for single-supply operation

dsp_model.description: Low-frequency oscillator generating triangle and square waveforms for modulation effects

parameters.sine_approximation: triangle waveform can be softened to approximate sine by applying tanh(tri * 1.5) / tanh(1.5)

behavioral_description: A classic op-amp triangle wave LFO that generates both triangle and square wave outputs simultaneously. The circuit uses a Schmitt trigger (comparator with positive feedback) to generate a square wave, which is then integrated to produce a clean triangle wave. The rate pot controls how fast the integrator charges, setting the LFO speed from slow, deep sweeps (0.5Hz) to fast, bubbly warble (15Hz). The triangle output is smooth and continuous, ideal for tremolo and phaser modulation. The square output provides abrupt on/off switching, useful for choppy tremolo or tap-tempo sync. By passing the triangle through a soft-clipping function (tanh), a good sine approximation can be derived for smoother, more subtle modulation. This LFO block is the modulation source for tremolo, vibrato, chorus, phaser, and flanger effects.
